cot_prefix: Okay, let's see. The question is asking why the text uses a lot of figures. The options are (A) record the valuable data, (B) provide concrete evidence, (C) introduce a research method, or (D) give a detailed description. First, I need to understand what each option means. "Record the valuable data" sounds like just storing information, but the text is more about using the data to support points. "Provide concrete evidence" would mean using the numbers to back up claims or arguments. "Introduce a research method" would involve explaining how the research was conducted, like surveys or experiments, which the text doesn't really do here. "Give a detailed description" would be about elaborating on something with specifics, but the figures here are more to support the main points. Looking at the text, the author uses percentages and growth rates to support the statements made by Rui Yao. For example, when discussing the growth rate of credit card adoption, they mention 40% per year. Then, when talking about the implications, they cite 90% of non-users unaware of safety issues and 60% with little knowledge of consequences. These numbers are used to back up the arguments about the need for financial education and responsible debt use. So the figures are serving as evidence to support the claims being made. Therefore, the correct answer is likely (B) provide concrete evidence. The
